euler_problems: Problem 99 - Largest Exponential: Given 1000 base/exponent pairs, determine which line has the greatest numerical value.
explanation: The code reads pairs of base and exponent values from a specified text file, processes them to determine their prime factors using a sieve method, and defines a function 'isAbiggerthanB' that compares two pairs of base and exponent. It iteratively compares the pairs and retains the one that represents the larger number until only one pair remains. Finally, it prints out the winning pair along with its original index from the input list.

files.content:
import copy

#read
lines = open(r"C:\Python27\Scripts\p099_base_exp.txt").readlines()
base_exp = [[int(i) for i in line.strip().split(',')] for line in lines]
base_exp_orig = [[int(i) for i in line.strip().split(',')] for line in lines]
all_numbers = [item for sublist in base_exp for item in sublist]
N = max_number = max(all_numbers)

#prime sieve + decomposition
numbers_decoposition = dict((k, []) for k in all_numbers)
sieve = [0] * (N+1)
primes = [2]
for i in xrange(3,N+1,2):
    if sieve[i] == 0:
        sieve[i] = 1
        primes.append(i)
        for j in xrange(i, N+1, i):
            sieve[j]=1
            if j in numbers_decoposition:
                numbers_decoposition[j].append(i)

print "done"


def isAbiggerthanB(A,B):

    a = copy.deepcopy(A)
    b = copy.deepcopy(B)
    while (True):

        #print "a:",a, "b:", b
        if (a[1] <= 10 and b[1] <=10):
            return a[0]**a[1] > b[0]**b[1]

        if (a[1] == 0 or b[1] == 0):
            return a[0]**a[1] > b[0]**b[1]
        
        # a[0]**a[1] ==?== b[0]**b[1]
        if (a[1] > b[1]):
            c = a[1] - b[1]
            # a[0]**(b[1]+c) ==?== b[0]**b[1]
            # a[0]**(c) ==?== (b[0]/a[0])**b[1]
            a[1]=c
            b[0]=b[0]/float(a[0])
            continue

        else:
             c = b[1] - a[1]
             # a[0]**(a[1]) ==?== b[0]**(a[1]+c)
             # (a[0]/b[0])**(a[1]) ==?== b[0]**(c)
             #return isAbiggerthanB(((a[0]/float(b[0])),a[1]),((b[0]),c))
             a[0]=a[0]/float(b[0])
             b[1]=c
             continue


a = base_exp[0]
b = base_exp[1]
print isAbiggerthanB(a,b)

while len(base_exp) > 1:

    print "="*80
    print "a:",a, "b:", b
    if isAbiggerthanB(base_exp[0],base_exp[1]):
        base_exp.pop(1)
    else:
        base_exp.pop(0)
    print "="*20, "size:", len(base_exp)

print "the winner is:", base_exp,"in index:", base_exp_orig.index(base_exp[0])

summary: This project implements an algorithm to determine which of a set of base-exponent pairs yields a larger number based on the values of the bases and exponents given in a file.
